overview.description: AI-powered legal drafting and document automation platform for law firms and in-house legal teams
overview.description_long: Avvoka provides drafting infrastructure for modern legal teams. Founded by two former Magic Circle lawyers, the platform combines advanced document automation, AI-assisted drafting, and human-in-the-loop control. It turns firm precedents into reusable, intelligent templates — enabling teams to manage high-volume, high-variation work without losing consistency, governance, or commercial oversight. Trusted by over 20% of the Am Law 100 including A&O Shearman, Fenwick, Fried Frank, and Ropes & Gray, as well as in-house legal teams at Warner Bros. Discovery and other multinationals.
overview.tagline: Drafting infrastructure for modern legal teams

history.origin_story: Founded in 2016 by two former Magic Circle lawyers who experienced first-hand the friction between speed and precision in legal drafting. They set out to separate legal thinking from document format and turn firm precedents into reusable, intelligent systems.

key_milestones.event: Platform launched; £600k raised from angel investors
Mark O'Hare joins board of directors
Raised £14 million growth investment led by Valhalla Ventures (Mark & Lindy O'Hare); adopted by 20%+ of Am Law 100

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Avvoka embeds AI within rules-based automation frameworks built on firm-specific precedents — making it harder for generic LLMs to replicate without institutional knowledge. However, as foundational legal AI models improve, the document automation layer could face commoditisation pressure; the platform's moat depends on governance, workflow integrations, and switching costs.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Legal document automation is a well-established, fast-growing market with strong PMF: over 20% of Am Law 100 already use Avvoka, and clients report 95% reduction in drafting time. Revenue and headcount growing 70%+ YoY before institutional funding confirms demand.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Just raised £14M growth round in March 2026 led by Valhalla Ventures after a decade of near-bootstrapped operation. The company has a proven revenue model and enterprise customer base before taking institutional capital.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Avvoka's deep integration into law firm workflows, firm-specific precedent systems, and strong enterprise client base across the Am Law 100 create substantial switching costs. Recent £14M funding provides runway for US expansion, and the product has clear ROI for buyers in a large, underserved market.

metadata.notes: Company incorporated August 2015 per Companies House (reg. 09729807); product launched and established 2016. Offices confirmed: London (HQ), Prague (Czech Republic), Singapore, Manila. £14M growth round announced March 2026, first institutional capital after £600k angel round in 2016. Prague office has 5 open engineering/AI roles.
